title: DotShare OAuth UI Refinements & Bundle Optimization
description: Implemented Advanced OAuth UI components (Connect/Disconnect toggles, connected badges) and optimized the VSIX build size.

completed_in_2_3_0.title: Seamless OAuth 2.0 — Auth Server + Extension Integration
completed_in_2_3_0.description: Full one-click OAuth flow: browser opens auth server → user clicks once → token delivered automatically to VS Code via deep link.
auth_server_changes: Removed all credential input forms (LinkedIn, X, Facebook, Reddit auth pages)
All app secrets moved exclusively to server .env — zero client-side exposure
All API routes (/api/auth/*) read credentials from process.env only
All callback pages now auto-redirect via vscode://freerave.dotshare/auth after 1.5s
sessionStorage limited to non-secret PKCE verifier and CSRF state only
extension_changes: Added URI Handler in extension.ts for vscode://freerave.dotshare/auth deep link
Handler stores tokens directly in context.secrets per platform automatically
Handler triggers webview loadConfiguration refresh after token save
Added success notification with 'Open DotShare' action button
platforms_completed: X         — OAuth 2.0 PKCE          → stores: xAccessToken + xRefreshToken
Reddit    — OAuth 2.0 + CSRF State  → stores: redditAccessToken + redditRefreshToken

completed_in_2_4_0.title: OAuth UI Refinements, Bundle Optimization & Direct Platform APIs
completed_in_2_4_0.description: Enhanced user experience for OAuth connection management, reduced VS Code extension bundle size, and fully migrated all platforms to post directly to their respective APIs.
ui_improvements: Added Connected ✓ badges and green states for all platforms
Created Disconnect buttons to easily clear secrets
Wrapped manual token inputs in an 'Advanced' collapsible accordion
All platforms (LinkedIn, X, Facebook, Reddit) use identical unified UI
build_optimizations: Updated .vscodeignore to exclude dev dependencies (e.g. __tests__, src/)
Reduced final .vsix bundle size significantly by excluding unnecessary config files
Verified clean builds for both tsc and esbuild
direct_api_migrations_completed: LinkedIn: Migrated to post directly to LinkedIn API using linkedinToken.
X/Twitter: Migrated to post directly to Twitter API v2 using xAccessToken.
Facebook: Enabled shareToFacebookWithUpdate and post directly to Graph API.
Reddit: Rewrote reddit.ts to post directly to oauth.reddit.com/api/submit.
PostHandler: Wired up all platform platform SDKs to use context.secrets.
hashtag_logic_fixes: HashtagService: Added HASHTAG_UNSUPPORTED_PLATFORMS set (reddit, discord) and supportsHashtags(platform) method.
gemini.ts / openai.ts / xai.ts / claude.ts: All 4 AI providers now skip hashtag appending when platform is reddit or discord.
HashtagContext: Added optional projectName? field — populated from package.json name or Cargo.toml name, with workspace folder basename as fallback.
HashtagService.extractKeywords(): Refactored to return { keywords, projectName } instead of a plain string[]. projectName no longer mixed into keywords array.
HashtagService.getProjectHashtags(): Now prefers context.projectName over guessing from keywords (prevents random keywords like 'test' or 'lint' becoming hashtags).
contextBuilder.ts: HashtagContext now receives projectData.projectName directly.

HashtagService.getContentBasedHashtags(): Split regex simplified from /[^a-zA-Z0-9.\-/]/ to /[^a-zA-Z0-9]/ — techTerms updated accordingly (ci/cd → cicd, node.js → nodejs), and the redundant cleanTerm step removed.
package.json: Registered dotshare.customHashtags (array) and dotshare.defaultPlatform (enum) in contributes.configuration so both settings appear in VS Code's built-in Settings UI.

services.name: ThreadService.ts
services.purpose: Manage multi-part post creation and validation

services.name: WebhookService.ts
services.purpose: Handle custom API endpoint payloads and authentication

handlers.name: TwitterHandler.ts
handlers.purpose: Implement thread posting logic via X API

ui.name: ThreadBuilderUI
ui.purpose: UI component for chaining multiple posts

x_twitter_threads: Update UI to allow adding 'Next Post' to create a chain.
Implement character count validation per thread item.
Update `TwitterHandler` to use `in_reply_to_tweet_id` for sequential posting.
custom_webhooks: Add 'Custom API' option to platform selector.
UI for configuring Webhook URL, Headers (Auth), and Payload mapping.
Implement `WebhookService` to fire POST requests with post content.
enhanced_progress_indicators: Implement real-time progress bars for media uploads in UI.
Add CLI progress spinners/bars for large files.

memory_optimization: Stream large media files during upload instead of loading entirely into memory.
Implement garbage collection triggers after large video/GIF uploads.

phase_0_fix_posting: Fix linkedin.ts to post directly to LinkedIn API.
Fix x.ts to use xAccessToken from context.secrets.
Fix facebook.ts and enable shareToFacebookWithUpdate in PostHandler.
Fix reddit.ts to post directly to Reddit API.
phase_1_threads: Update X (Twitter) integration to support sequential posting.
phase_2_webhooks: Create UI configuration for Custom Webhooks.
phase_3_media_optimization: Add visual progress indicators to UI and CLI.

feature_completion: Successful payload delivery to a custom webhook.
All 4 platforms (LinkedIn, X, Facebook, Reddit) posting directly via their APIs.
performance: No memory spikes during >10MB media uploads.
Real-time progress indicators working for all media uploads.
